clinical_criteria: TG levels must be <4.5 mmol/L (400 mg/dL) for Friedewald equation
Remove measurements outside +/- 6 SD from overall mean
Friedewald equation for LDL-C calculation: LDL-C = Total Cholesterol - HDL-C - (TG/2.2) when values in mmol/L
Friedewald equation for LDL-C calculation: LDL-C = Total Cholesterol - HDL-C - (TG/5) when values in mg/dL
Adjusted LDL-C formula: LDLC_adjusted = LDLC / 0.7 (when on lipid-lowering medication)
algorithm_summary: This is a 7-step harmonization algorithm for lipids phenotyping: 1) Extract measurements using codes from CSV files (HDL_C_Codes.csv, TG_Codes.csv, LDL_C_Codes.csv), 2) Convert units to mmol/L, 3) Apply exclusion criteria (age <18, inpatient/ED visits), 4) Prepare measurements (include fasting/non-fasting, apply medication adjustment for LDL-C if applicable), 5) Remove statistical outliers (±6 SD), 6) Compute single value per individual using earliest timepoint, 7) Flag individuals with conditions like Cushing's syndrome, hypothyroidism, T2DM, etc. The algorithm uses ATC code C10 for lipid-lowering medications and can calculate LDL-C using Friedewald equation when direct measurement unavailable.
